travelsupermarket.name: search-holidays
travelsupermarket.description: Search for package holiday offers. Optionally filter by destination name or ID (e.g. "Tenerife", "Majorca", "Spain") — the destination is resolved automatically. If no destination is given, returns top deals across all destinations. Returns hotel details, prices (in GBP), departure info, and booking links. A specific departure date is NOT required — you can search by departure month(s) instead (e.g. "6" for June, "6,7,8" for summer). When the user mentions a month or time of year, use the departureMonth parameter directly rather than picking an arbitrary date. IMPORTANT: when the user mentions travelling with children/kids, ask a clarifying question to check whether any of them are under 2 years old (24 months) before calling this tool — under-2s must be passed as `infants`, while everyone aged 2–17 is a `child`. Do not assume; ask if it is unclear.

destination.description: Destination name (e.g. "Tenerife", "Majorca", "Spain") or numeric destination ID. Optional — omit to search across all destinations.

departureMonth.description: Comma-separated month numbers 1-12 (e.g. "6,7,8" for summer). Use this instead of a specific date — no start date is needed. When the user says "in June" pass "6", "this summer" pass "6,7,8", etc.

boardBasis.description: Comma-separated codes: AI (All-inclusive), BB (B&B), FB (Full Board), HB (Half Board), RO (Room Only), SC (Self Catering)

departureAirport.description: Comma-separated IATA airport codes — e.g. "MAN" (Manchester), "LGW" (Gatwick), "LHR" (Heathrow), "STN" (Stansted), "BHX" (Birmingham), "EDI" (Edinburgh), "BRS" (Bristol), "LBA" (Leeds Bradford), "NCL" (Newcastle), "GLA" (Glasgow). Always use the 3-letter IATA code, not the airport name.

children.description: Number of children aged 2-17 (e.g. "3"). Defaults to 0. Must be set explicitly — if the user says "2 adults" with no mention of children, pass "0" to avoid getting family results. Anyone under 2 years old (24 months) belongs in `infants`, not here — if the user mentions kids without specifying ages, ask whether any are under 2 years old before calling.

infants.description: Number of infants under 2 years old / 24 months (e.g. "1"). Defaults to 0. Children aged 2 and over go in `children`. If the user mentions babies/young kids without confirming age, ask before assuming.

facilities.description: Comma-separated facility IDs to filter hotels. Known IDs: 1=Swimming pool, 2=Close to beach, 3=Air-conditioning, 4=Internet access, 5=Balcony/terrace, 6=Kids club, 7=Restaurant(s), 8=Children's pool, 9=Wheelchair access, 10=Nightclub, 11=Bar(s), 12=Spa facilities, 13=Playground, 14=Pool bar, 15=Sun loungers, 16=Entertainment programme, 17=Water sports, 18=Sports facilities, 19=Gym, 20=Jacuzzi, 21=Sun terrace, 22=Games room, 23=Golf course, 24=Shopping opportunities. Example: "1,12,19" for pool + spa + gym.

brandId.description: Comma-separated provider/brand IDs to filter by holiday company. Known providers: 1224=TUI, 1250=OnTheBeach, 1251=LoveHolidays, 1258=Easyjet Holidays, 1275=Jet2holidays, 1563=Holiday Gems, 1632=Blue Bay Travel. Example: "1275" for Jet2holidays only, or "1224,1258" for TUI and Easyjet.

maxPrice.description: Maximum per-person price in GBP (e.g. 500 for £500pp)

theme.description: Holiday theme: luxury, familyFriendly, lastMinute, closeToBeach, winterSun, shortBreaks

travelsupermarket.name: search-car-hire
travelsupermarket.description: Search for car hire deals by pickup location. Accepts city names or IATA airport codes (e.g. "Malaga", "Venice", "AGP", "VCE"). Use the city name or 3-letter IATA code — do NOT append "Airport". Optionally specify a different dropoff location for one-way rentals. Returns vehicle options with prices in GBP, specs, and booking links. IMPORTANT: driver age has a real impact on price and eligibility (under-25s and over-70s often see surcharges or vehicle restrictions). Always ask the user for the main driver's age before calling this tool — do not silently fall back to the default. Only skip the question if the user has already volunteered their age in the conversation.

pickupLocation.description: Pickup location — city name or IATA airport code. Use "Malaga" or "AGP", NOT "Malaga Airport". Examples: "Venice", "VCE", "Malaga", "AGP", "Tenerife"

dropoffLocation.description: Dropoff location — omit for round-trip (same as pickup). Provide for one-way rentals.

driverAge.description: Main driver's age in years. Ask the user for this — it materially affects price and eligibility (young drivers under 25 and seniors over 70 commonly attract surcharges or are excluded from some vehicle categories). Only fall back to the default of 30 if the user has explicitly said they don't know or don't want to specify.

category.description: Comma-separated car categories: Small, Medium, Large, SUV, Estate, People Carrier, Convertible, Luxury, Van. Example: "Small,Medium"

fuelPolicy.description: Fuel policy filter: "F2F" (Full to Full), "F2E" (Full to Empty), "S2S" (Same to Same)

policies.description: Comma-separated policy filters: cancel (Free cancellation), mileage (Unlimited mileage), additionalDriver (Free additional driver), breakdownAssistance (Breakdown assistance). Example: "cancel,mileage"

maxPrice.description: Maximum total price in GBP (e.g. 300 for £300 total)

supplier.description: Filter by car supplier/rental company name (e.g. "Hertz", "Sixt")

arrival.description:
The arrival date in YYYY-MM-DD format, today is 2026-06-09.

		The arrival date MUST be before the departure date. If the arrival date is not in the future, notify the user that the arrival date is not in the future.

children_ages.description: Dashed separated list of children ages, e.g. 10-12-14

departure.description:
The departure date in YYYY-MM-DD format, today is 2026-06-09.

		The departure date MUST be after the arrival date. If the departure date is not after the arrival date, notify the user that the departure date is not after the arrival date.

filters.description:
The filters that you want to apply to the search.
		Set true or false for each filter.
		Multiple filters can be selected.

airConditioning.description: Whether the accommodation has air conditioning

freeCancellation.description: Whether the accommodation has free cancellation

freeWiFi.description: Whether the accommodation has free WiFi

gym.description: Whether the accommodation has a gym

kitchen.description: Whether the accommodation has a kitchen

petFriendly.description: Whether the accommodation is pet friendly

pool.description: Whether the accommodation has a pool

spa.description: Whether the accommodation has a spa

hotel_rating.description:
The hotel rating that you want to filter by.
		Set true or false for each rating.
		Multiple ratings can be selected.

1star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 1 star rating

2star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 2 star rating

3star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 3 star rating

4star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 4 star rating

5star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 5 star rating

latitude.description: The latitude of the search target location (e.g. a landmark or address), not the user's personal location.

longitude.description: The longitude of the search target location (e.g. a landmark or address), not the user's personal location.

radius.description: The radius in meters that you want to search for.

review_rating.description:
The guest review rating that you want to filter by.
		Set true or false for each rating.
		Multiple ratings can be selected.

rating70.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 7.0+

rating75.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 7.5+

rating80.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 8.0+

rating85.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 8.5+

rooms.description: The number of rooms, Number of rooms must be lower than or equal to the number of adults

trivago.name: trivago-accommodation-radius-search

accommodations.description: The list of accommodations. Display each one as a separate visual card with image, not as a table.

accommodation_url.description: URL to the accommodation page on Trivago.

booking_url.description: Direct URL to book the accommodation.

distance_to_city_center.description: Distance to the city center with value and unit.

unit.description: The unit of the distance to the city center.

value.description: The distance value to the city center.

main_image.description: URL of the main image of the accommodation.

error.description: The error if the tool failed to execute.

system_message.description: MANDATORY formatting instructions. You MUST read and follow these instructions exactly to format the accommodations. Do NOT use a comparison table — display each hotel as a separate card with its image.

validation_errors.description: The validation errors if the tool failed to execute.

argument.description: The argument that caused the error.

value.description: The value that caused the error.

arrival.description:
The arrival date in YYYY-MM-DD format, today is 2026-06-09.

		The arrival date MUST be before the departure date. If the arrival date is not in the future, notify the user that the arrival date is not in the future.

children_ages.description: Dashed separated list of children ages, e.g. 10-12-14

departure.description:
The departure date in YYYY-MM-DD format, today is 2026-06-09.

		The departure date MUST be after the arrival date. If the departure date is not after the arrival date, notify the user that the departure date is not after the arrival date.

filters.description:
The filters that you want to apply to the search.
		Set true or false for each filter.
		Multiple filters can be selected.

airConditioning.description: Whether the accommodation has air conditioning

freeCancellation.description: Whether the accommodation has free cancellation

freeWiFi.description: Whether the accommodation has free WiFi

gym.description: Whether the accommodation has a gym

kitchen.description: Whether the accommodation has a kitchen

petFriendly.description: Whether the accommodation is pet friendly

pool.description: Whether the accommodation has a pool

spa.description: Whether the accommodation has a spa

hotel_rating.description:
The hotel rating that you want to filter by.
		Set true or false for each rating.
		Multiple ratings can be selected.

1star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 1 star rating

2star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 2 star rating

3star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 3 star rating

4star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 4 star rating

5star.description: Whether the accommodation has a 5 star rating

id.description: The ID of the location that you want to search for.

ns.description: The NS of the location that you want to search for.

review_rating.description:
The guest review rating that you want to filter by.
		Set true or false for each rating.
		Multiple ratings can be selected.

rating70.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 7.0+

rating75.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 7.5+

rating80.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 8.0+

rating85.description: Whether the accommodation has a Guest Rating of 8.5+

rooms.description: The number of rooms, Number of rooms must be lower than or equal to the number of adults

trivago.name: trivago-accommodation-search

accommodations.description: The list of accommodations. Display each one as a separate visual card with image, not as a table.

accommodation_url.description: URL to the accommodation page on Trivago.

booking_url.description: Direct URL to book the accommodation.

distance_to_city_center.description: Distance to the city center with value and unit.

unit.description: The unit of the distance to the city center.

value.description: The distance value to the city center.

main_image.description: URL of the main image of the accommodation.

error.description: The error if the tool failed to execute.

system_message.description: MANDATORY formatting instructions. You MUST read and follow these instructions exactly to format the accommodations. Do NOT use a comparison table — display each hotel as a separate card with its image.

validation_errors.description: The validation errors if the tool failed to execute.

argument.description: The argument that caused the error.

value.description: The value that caused the error.

trivago.description:
Suggestions are used to provide a list of possible search terms based on the user's query.

	Query can be city, country.

	You must pick output that are close to the user query.

	Example:
	Input:
		Query: "Berlin"
		Query: "Germany"

query.description:
The query to search for suggestions.

		Query must be city, country.

		if you know geolocation, you can use radius search tool to find accommodations near the location.

		if query or the location is ambiguous, clarify the query or location by asking the user for more information.

		When user ask for a query, you must follow these steps. If each step is not successful, try the next step:
		   1. first try to use query as it is
		   2. MUST find the city of the query by using internet search, use MUST the city to search for suggestions
		   3. MUST find the country of the query by using internet search, use MUST the country to search for suggestions

trivago.name: trivago-search-suggestions

error.description: The error message if the tool failed to execute.

id.description: The ID of the suggestion. It's used to search for the accommodation.

ns.description: The NS of the suggestion. It's used to search for the accommodation.

place_id.description: The place ID of the suggestion.

system_message.description: The system message to AI/LLM to understand the task that you must to do.

kiwi.name: search-flight
kiwi.description:
# Search for a flight

## Description

Uses the Kiwi API to search for available flights between two locations on a specific date.

## How it works

The tool will:
1. Search for matching locations to resolve airport codes
2. Find available flights for the specified route and date range

## Method

Call this tool whenever a user wants to search for flights, regardless of whether they provided exact airport codes or just city names.

You should display the returned results in a markdown table format: Group the results by price (those who are the cheapest), duration (those who are the shortest, i.e. have the smallest 'totalDurationInSeconds') and the rest (those that could still be interesting).

Always display for each flight in order:
  - In the 1st column: The departure and arrival airports, including layovers (e.g. "Paris CDG → Barcelona BCN → Lisbon LIS")
  - In the 2nd column: The departure and arrival dates & times in the local timezones, and duration of the flight (e.g. "03/08 06:05 → 09:30 (3h 25m)", use 'durationInSeconds' to display the duration and not 'totalDurationInSeconds')
  - In the 3rd column: The cabin class (e.g. "Economy")
  - (In case of return flight only) In the 4th column: The return flight departure and arrival airports, including layovers (e.g. "Paris CDG → Barcelona BCN → Lisbon LIS")
  - (In case of return flight only) In the 5th column: The return flight departure and arrival dates & times in the local timezones, and duration of the flight (e.g. "03/08 06:05 → 09:30 (3h 25m)", use 'return.durationInSeconds' to display the duration)
  - (In case of return flight only) In the 6th column: The return flight cabin class (e.g. "Economy")
  - In the previous-to-last column: The total price of the flight
  - In the last column: The deep link to book the flight

Finally, provide a summary highlighting the best prices, the shortest flights and a recommendation. End wishing a nice trip to the user with a short fun fact about the destination!

flyFrom.description: Location to fly from: It could be a city or an airport name or code

flyTo.description: Location to fly to: It could be a city or an airport name or code

departureDateFlexRange.description: Departure date flexibility range in days (0 to 3 days before/after the selected departure date)

returnDateFlexRange.description: Return date flexibility range in days (0 to 3 days before/after the selected return date)

adults.description: Number of adults (over 12 years old included)

children.description: Number of children (from 3 to 11 years old included)

passengers.description: Passengers details. The total number of passengers must be between 1 and 9. There must be at least one adult. There must be at least one adult per infant.

cabinClass.description: Cabin class: M (economy), W (economy premium), C (business), F (first class)

sort.description: Sort results by: price, duration, quality or date (default: date)

curr.description: Currency for response (examples: EUR, USD, GBP, JPY, CAD, AUD, NZD, CHF etc.)

locale.description: Language of city names and kiwi.com website links (examples: en, uk, de, fr, es, it, ru etc.)

kiwi.name: feedback-to-devs
kiwi.description: Send feedback to the dev of the Kiwi MCP server.

text.description: The content of the feedback. Don't hesitate to include any text relevant to the issue (logs, error message) if you are having one.

annotations.title: Send feedback to the devs of the Kiwi.com MCP server

economybookings.name: search-car-hire
economybookings.description: Search for car hire deals worldwide by pickup location. Accepts city names or IATA airport codes (e.g. "Malaga", "Venice", "AGP", "VCE"). Returns vehicle options with prices, supplier ratings, and booking links. IMPORTANT: driver age has a real impact on price and eligibility — EconomyBookings prices best for drivers aged 30-65; under-30s and over-65s commonly attract young/senior driver surcharges or vehicle restrictions. Always ask the user for the main driver's age before calling this tool — do not silently fall back to the default. Only skip the question if the user has already volunteered their age in the conversation.

pickupLocation.description: Pickup location — city name or IATA airport code. Use "Malaga" or "AGP", NOT "Malaga Airport".

dropoffLocation.description: Dropoff location — omit for round-trip (same as pickup). Provide for one-way rentals.

pickupDate.description: Pickup date as YYYY-MM-DD. MUST be today or later — past dates are rejected. Before calling the tool, sanity-check the date against TODAY (which is in your system context). If the user says a bare month/day with no year (e.g. 'June 15'), interpret it as the next future occurrence. If the user explicitly states a past year (e.g. 'June 15, 2024'), DO NOT call the tool — first ask whether they meant the next future year (e.g. 2026) and confirm before proceeding.

dropoffDate.description: Dropoff date as YYYY-MM-DD. MUST be on or after pickupDate, and on or after today. Same year-resolution rules as pickupDate: prefer the next future occurrence for ambiguous dates and confirm with the user before calling the tool with any explicitly-past date.

driverAge.description: Driver's age (years). **DO NOT ASK THE USER FOR THIS — it is friction we explicitly want to avoid.** Leave the field blank by default; the server returns prices computed for a 35-year-old (the no-surcharge mid-band), which is the right answer for the vast majority of users. Only populate this field when (a) the user volunteered their age in this conversation without being asked, or (b) you already have it from prior context / memory you have on the user. Do NOT ask the user to provide their age just because surcharges might apply — they can correct us afterwards if needed.

category.description: Comma-separated car categories. Substring match — e.g. "Luxury" matches both "Luxury" and "Luxury Elite". Valid values from EconomyBookings docs §6.3: Mini, Mini Elite, Economy, Economy Elite, Compact, Compact Elite, Intermediate, Intermediate Elite, Standard, Standard Elite, Fullsize, Fullsize Elite, Premium, Premium Elite, Luxury, Luxury Elite, Oversize, Special, Van, Electric, Midsize, SUV, Estate, Convertible, Small.

maxPrice.description: Maximum total price in the customer currency (e.g. 300 for £300 total).

locale.description: User's preferred language as a BCP 47 tag (e.g. 'en-GB', 'es-ES', 'fr-FR', 'de-DE', 'pt-BR'). Infer from the language the user is writing in — if they message in Spanish, pass 'es-ES'; in German, 'de-DE'; etc. Used to localise prices and to redirect them to the matching version of the booking site. Skip when the conversation language matches the host's UI language (we'll pick it up from there).

country.description: User's CURRENT country (where they physically are right now) as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (e.g. 'GB', 'ES', 'US', 'KR', 'JP'). Drives the currency and pricing market we ask EB for. NOT the trip destination they're searching for. Priority order for inferring this: (1) the user's approximate location provided in the system context — if present, use that country; (2) timezone if known; (3) what the user has explicitly said in this conversation about where they are right now. Do NOT use long-term memory of where the user usually lives — they may be travelling. Do NOT use the trip destination. If sources conflict (e.g. memory says one country, system location says another), trust the system location — the user is likely travelling. Skip if no signal at all — we'll fall back to other location detectors.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP', 'KRW'). Pass this ONLY when the user explicitly asks for prices in a specific currency (e.g. 'show me the price in USD'). Otherwise leave blank — the server picks the right currency from the user's country. Overrides the country-derived currency.

supplier.description: Optional supplier filter — pass the supplier name (or a unique substring) when the user asks for a specific car-hire company (e.g. 'ClickRent', 'Hertz', 'GoldCar', 'Europcar'). Case-insensitive substring match against the supplier list. If we can't find that supplier we'll surface the closest available alternatives.

vehicleModel.description: Optional vehicle make / model filter — pass it when the user mentions a specific car (e.g. 'Toyota Corolla', 'Fiat 500', 'BMW 1 Series'). Case-insensitive substring match against the vehicle name. If the exact model isn't available we'll surface the closest alternatives.

tourradar.name: b2b-operator-details
tourradar.title: Operator Details
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user wants information about a tour operator, such as their terms and policies.

Fetches operator details including name, code, and terms & conditions based on operator ID.

tourradar.name: b2b-cities-search
tourradar.title: Cities search
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need to find city IDs for tour search filters like start city, end city, or cities to visit.

Searches for cities by name within a specific country. Supports multiple name variants to handle different spellings (e.g., Krakow, Kraków, Cracow).

country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., 'US' for USA, 'GB' for United Kingdom, 'DE' for Germany).

search.description: City name filter. It is recommended to use this filter to narrow down the search results. Please use english only names. How to search: If you are looking for Kraków city, please pass potential candidates here, like: ['Krakow', 'Kraków', 'Cracow']. It will return all cities that match any of the names.

tourradar.name: b2b-continents-list
tourradar.title: Continents list
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need continent IDs for filtering tours by region.

Returns a list of all supported continents with their IDs and names for use in tour search filters.

continents.description: List of the continents with their IDs and names.

tourradar.name: b2b-countries-list
tourradar.title: Countries list
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need to look up country IDs for filtering tours or validating country names.

Returns a complete list of all supported countries with their IDs, names, and ISO country codes.

country_code.description: Country code like US, RU, FR, ES

tourradar.name: b2b-currencies-list
tourradar.title: Currencies list
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need to check supported currencies or get currency details like symbols.

Returns a list of all supported currencies with their codes (USD, EUR), names, and symbols ($, €).

code.description: Currency code like USD, EUR, AUD

name.description: Currency name like Australian Dollar, Euro, Pound Sterling

tourradar.name: b2b-languages-list
tourradar.title: Languages list
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need language IDs for filtering tours by guide language.

Returns a list of all supported languages with their IDs, codes, and names for use in tour search filters.

code.description: Language code like ru, en, fr, es

name.description: Language name like Russian, English, French, Spanish

languages.description: List of supported languages with their IDs

tourradar.name: b2b-tour-types-list
tourradar.title: Tour Types List
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need tour type IDs for filtering tours by category like adventure, cultural, or wildlife.

Returns a hierarchical list of tour type groups and their individual types with IDs and names.

children.description: Tour types within the the group, if any

tourradar.name: b2b-tour-departures
tourradar.title: Tour Departures
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user asks about available departure dates, pricing for specific dates, or needs to validate if a departure date is available.

Returns a list of departures for a specific tour within a date range, including availability status, pricing, and booking information.

dateRange.description: Returns only departure dates in the desired range

currency.description: Currency to return prices in. Use this only when the user explicitly asks for a specific currency. Otherwise the user's default (based on location) is used.

dateRange.description: Returns only departure dates in the desired range

id.description: Identifier of the departure date (must be used for price calculation and booking creation)

date.description: Date of the departure, in the format `YYYY-MM-DD`

availability.description: Number of available seats for the specific departure

departure_type.description: Type of departure: guaranteed, scheduled, or on request

is_instant_confirmable.description: If true, bookings related to this departure can be instantly confirmed by the operator

guide_languages.description: List of languages this tour is offered in

type.description: Type of link: tour page or booking page

currency.description: Currency code for the reported prices

price_base.description: Base price of the departure, also referenced as brochure price

price_addons.description: Sum of the mandatory addons that the passenger will have to pay (both upfront and local payments)

price_promotion.description: Discounted price, or base price minus the promotion

price_total.description: Final sale price, composed by the base price, minus the applied promotion, plus the mandatory local and upfront addons

price_total_upfront.description: Final sale price, composed by the base price, minus the applied promotion, plus the upfront addons

id.description: Unique identifier of the promotion in the TourRadar system

discount.description: Amount of the promotion (to subtract from the base price)

type.description: Type of the promotion (percentage discount, or 2-for-1 offer)

percentage.description: Amount of the promotion, when promotion is based on percentage discount

pax_restriction.description: Maximum pax number to apply this promotion. If null - no limit

value_type.description: Type of the value, can be absolute or percent

valid_to.description: Validity definition - date until when the promotion is valid

based_on.description: If available, the type of accommodation that the base price refers to

mandatory_addons.description: List of the mandatory addons (extra price elements)

payment_type.description: Payment type: upfront or local payment

local_currency.description: The used currency for local payment

next_page.description: If this attribute exists, then another page is available. To get more results, repeat the request with the number specified in the `page` parameter

departure_months.description: Available departure months for the tour (included on page 1)

tourradar.name: b2b-tour-details
tourradar.title: Tour Details
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user wants to see detailed information about a specific tour.

Fetches comprehensive tour details including itinerary, pricing, operator info, images, and booking links based on tour ID.

currency.description: Currency to return prices in. Use this only when the user explicitly asks for a specific currency. Otherwise the user's default (based on location) is used.

tour_id.description: Unique identifier for the tour in the TourRadar system (please note that this is different from the operator code)

locale.description: Language locale the content is provided, default is `en_US` (English)

codes.description: Operator codes for the tour. Note that some operators offer the same tours in a number of variants, but TourRadar handles them as a single tour

language.description: Language code the content is provided, default is `en` (English)

tour_name.description: Name of the tour. Note that tour names are sometimes adjusted by TourRadar, and might not exactly match the name provided by the operator

is_active.description: Determines if the tour is currently active, e.g. if it has departure dates that can be booked

description.description: Description of the tour, providing a summary of the itinerary and the key important selling points for the tour

tour_length_days.description: Length of the tour, expressed in number of days

is_instant_confirmable.description: If true, bookings related to this tour can be instantly confirmed by the operator

guide_languages.description: List of languages this tour is offered in

start_point.description: Information about the starting point of the tour (e.g. pickup point), if available

end_point.description: Information about the ending point of the tour (e.g. drop off point), if available

budget.description: A market term that defines the price category of the tour

max_group_size.description: Maximum number of passengers allowed on the tour

id.description: Unique identifier of the operator in the TourRadar system

partner_info.description: Information about the partner-specific component of the price, including commission

contract_type.description: Type of contract with the partner

based_on.description: If available, the type of accommodation that the base price refers to

price_base.description: Base price of the tour, also referenced as brochure price. Note this is the lowest price among the possible departure dates

price_addons.description: Sum of the mandatory addons that the passenger will have to pay (both upfront and local payments)

price_promotion.description: Discounted price, or base price minus the promotion

price_total_upfront.description: Final sale price, composed by the base price, minus the applied promotion, plus the upfront addons

price_total.description: Final sale price, composed by the base price, minus the applied promotion, plus the mandatory local and upfront addons

currency.description: Currency code for the reported prices

discount_value.description: Amount of the promotion (to subtract from the base price)

discount_type.description: Type of the promotion: percentage discount or fixed amount

mandatory_addons.description: List of the mandatory addons (extra price elements)

payment_type.description: Payment type: upfront or local payment

local_currency.description: The used currency for local payment

order.description: Order of this item in the itinerary (day number)

duration.description: Duration of this itinerary item in days

description.description: Detailed description of activities for this day

location_id.description: Unique identifier for the city in the TourRadar system

country_code.description: ISO code for the country where the city is located

start_city.description: The city where the tour starts

location_id.description: Unique identifier for the city in the TourRadar system

country_code.description: ISO code for the country where the city is located

end_city.description: The city where the tour ends

location_id.description: Unique identifier for the city in the TourRadar system

country_code.description: ISO code for the country where the city is located

destinations.description: All destinations visited during the tour

location_id.description: Unique identifier for the city in the TourRadar system

country_code.description: ISO code for the country where the city is located

location_id.description: Unique identifier for the country in the TourRadar system

currency_code.description: Currency code used in the country

currency_name.description: Name of the currency used in the country

electricity_outlets.description: Types of electricity outlets used in the country

natural_destinations.description: List of the natural destinations related to the tour

location_id.description: Unique identifier for the natural destination

images.description: An array of images for the tour. This is limited to 3 entries, plus the image of the tour map

type.description: Type of image: regular image or tour map

tour_types.description: List of the types this tour is categorised under

type_id.description: Unique identifier for the tour type

services.description: This section describes the various services provided with the tour (accommodation, food, and so on)

is_included.description: Whether this service is included in the tour

nights.description: Number of nights at this accommodation

ship_rooms.description: Ship room details for cruise tours

type.description: Type of room on the ship

is_included.description: Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour
Whether this service is included in the tour

ratings.description: Ratings are collected by TourRadar from customers, after the tour is completed, and in some occasions provided by the operator

overall.description: Rating out of 5 for the overall tour

itinerary.description: Rating out of 5 for the itinerary of the tour

transport.description: Rating out of 5 for included transport services

food.description: Rating out of 5 for meals included in the tour

guide.description: Rating out of 5 for the guiding services of the tour

accommodation.description: Rating out of 5 for the included accommodation

operator.description: Rating out of 5 for the tour operator

reviews_count.description: Number of reviews related to this tour

strict.description: Strict age limits that must be followed

recommended.description: Recommended age range for the tour

has_machine_translated_content.description: This flag indicates if the content in the current language was translated automatically using a Machine Translation service

departure_months.description: List of available departure by month

thumbnail_template.description: Template for thumbnail. {size} is placeholder in this string, should be replaced with one of available sizes

moments.description: Short videos from the tour, in video/mp4 format

tourradar.name: b2b-tour-faq
tourradar.title: Tour FAQ
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user has questions about a tour that might be answered in the FAQ section.

Returns a paginated list of frequently asked questions and answers about a specific tour, covering topics like inclusions, requirements, and policies.

items.description: List of FAQ to the tour

tourradar.name: b2b-tour-map
tourradar.title: Tour Map
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user wants to see tour routes on a map or compare itineraries visually.

Fetches tour details for one or more tours and prepares map data with all itinerary locations, coordinates, and day-by-day information for visual display.

currency.description: Currency to return prices in. Use this only when the user explicitly asks for a specific currency. Otherwise the user's default (based on location) is used.

tourradar.name: general-current-date
tourradar.title: Current Date
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need to know the current date, especially before setting departure date filters.

Returns the current date and time in ISO format. Essential for calculating valid date ranges for tour searches.

tourradar.name: vertex-tour-search
tourradar.title: Tour Text Search
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user describes what they want in natural language and you need AI-powered semantic search to understand their intent.

Before use this tool, please READ all possible filters.
PLESE USE FILTERS, when can be used, to make search faster and much more precise.
Please use start_city, end_city, cities, countries, start_country, end_country filters if possible.  You can use multiple of them.

AI-powered semantic search for tours using natural language queries combined with optional filters. Uses Google Vertex AI to understand intent and find relevant tours based on descriptions, themes, or specific requests.

Use `vertex-tour-search` when:

- The user describes what they want in natural language
- You need semantic/AI-powered search to understand intent
- Combining natural language with filters for refined results

Examples:

- "Family-friendly safari with kids under 12"
- "Romantic honeymoon trip with beach and mountains"
- "Adventure tour with hiking and camping for beginners"
- "Cultural immersion experience with local homestays"
- "Wine tasting tour through European countryside"

## Input

### Required

- `textSearch`: Natural language description of what the user is looking for
- `display_mode`: How to display results — `"listing"` (default, carousel of tour cards) or `"map"` (interactive map view)

### Optional Filters

#### Location Filters

| Filter          | Type     | Description                                                                |
| --------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `start_country` | string[] | Country where tour BEGINS (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes). OR logic.            |
| `end_country`   | string[] | Country where tour ENDS (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes). OR logic.              |
| `start_city`    | number[] | City IDs where tour starts. Use `b2b-cities-search` to find IDs. OR logic. |
| `end_city`      | number[] | City IDs where tour ends. Use `b2b-cities-search` to find IDs. OR logic.   |
| `countries`     | object   | Countries visited DURING the itinerary. Supports AND/OR operator.          |
| `cities`        | object   | Cities visited on the itinerary. Supports AND/OR operator.                 |

#### Range Filters

| Filter           | Type                     | Description                                                                          |
| ---------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `duration`       | `{ min, max }`           | Tour length in days                                                                  |
| `max_group_size` | `{ min, max }`           | Maximum group size range                                                             |
| `min_group_size` | `{ min, max }`           | Minimum group size range                                                             |
| `min_age`        | `{ min, max }`           | Minimum age requirement range. E.g., `{ min: 1, max: 12 }` finds family-friendly tours |
| `max_age`        | `{ min, max }`           | Maximum age limit range. E.g., `{ min: 18, max: 39 }` finds youth-specific tours       |
| `price`          | `{ min, max, currency }` | Price range (currency: "EUR")                                                        |

#### AND/OR Filters

These filters support both AND and OR operators:

| Filter       | Values                   | Description                        |
| ------------ | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| `departures` | YYYY-MM strings          | Filter by departure months         |
| `countries`  | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes | Countries visited during itinerary |
| `cities`     | City IDs                 | Cities visited on itinerary        |

Structure: `{ values: [...], operator: "AND" | "OR" }`

- `OR` (default): Tour matches ANY of the specified values
- `AND`: Tour must match ALL specified values

## Examples

### Simple text search

```json
{ "textSearch": "family adventure with wildlife" }
```

### With location filters

```json
{
  "textSearch": "hiking adventure",
  "start_country": ["DE", "AT"],
  "countries": { "values": ["IT", "CH"], "operator": "AND" }
}
```

### With range filters

```json
{
  "textSearch": "luxury beach vacation",
  "duration": { "min": 7, "max": 14 },
  "price": { "min": 2000, "max": 5000, "currency": "EUR" },
  "max_group_size": { "min": 1, "max": 16 }
}
```

### With age filters

```json
{
  "textSearch": "family safari with young children",
  "min_age": { "min": 1, "max": 6 },
  "duration": { "min": 7, "max": 14 }
}
```

### With departure dates

```json
{
  "textSearch": "northern lights tour",
  "departures": { "values": ["2026-01", "2026-02", "2026-03"], "operator": "OR" }
}
```

### Map display mode

```json
{
  "textSearch": "hiking tours in the Alps",
  "display_mode": "map",
  "countries": { "values": ["AT", "CH"], "operator": "OR" }
}
```

## Response

Returns a list of tours matching the query, each containing:

- Tour ID, name, and URL
- Operator information
- Brief description matching the query context

tourIds.description: Filter by specific tour IDs. Use this to narrow search results to known tours.

display_mode.description: How to display the search results. 'listing' shows a carousel of tour cards. 'map' shows tours on an interactive map.

start_country.description: Filter by the country where the tour BEGINS (departure point). ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes (e.g., ['DE', 'AT']). Multiple values = OR logic. Use 'countries' filter for countries visited during the itinerary.

end_country.description: Filter by the country where the tour ENDS (final destination). ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes (e.g., ['IT', 'FR']). Multiple values = OR logic. Use 'countries' filter for countries visited during the itinerary.

start_city.description: City IDs where the tour starts (e.g., [1234, 5678]). Use b2b-cities-search to find city IDs. If passing multiple values, will find tours starting in any of the given cities

end_city.description: City IDs where the tour ends (e.g., [1234, 5678]). Use b2b-cities-search to find city IDs. If passing multiple values, will find tours ending in any of the given cities

duration.description: Filter tours by duration in days. Example: set min: 5 , max: 8 to find duration between those days. Include this filter when is needed

min.description: Minimum value for max group size

max.description: Maximum value for max group size

max_group_size.description: Filter by tour's maximum group size. E.g., { min: 1, max: 20 } finds small group tours with max 20 participants. Min and max value cannot be this same.

min.description: Minimum value for min group size

max.description: Maximum value for min group size

min_group_size.description: Filter by tour's minimum group size. E.g., { min: 1, max: 1 } finds tours that accept solo travelers. Min and max value cannot be this same.

min_age.description: Filter by tour's minimum age requirement. E.g., { min: 1, max: 12 } finds tours that accept children under 12. Use to find family-friendly tours or tours with low age requirements. Min and max value cannot be this same.

max_age.description: Filter by tour's maximum age limit. E.g., { min: 18, max: 39 } finds tours limited to young adults. Use to find age-restricted tours like youth or senior-specific trips. Min and max value cannot be this same.

price.description: Filter by tour price in the selected currency. E.g., { min: 500, max: 2000, currency: 'EUR' } finds tours priced between €500 and €2000. Min and max value cannot be this same.

discount.description: Filter tours by discount percentage. E.g., { min: 10, max: 50 } finds tours with 10% to 50% off. Min and max value cannot be the same.

operator.description: AND = tour must have departures in ALL specified months, OR = tour must have departures in ANY of the specified months

departures.description: Filter tours by available departure months

values.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes that the tour passes through (e.g., ["DE", "AT", "CH"])

operator.description: AND = tour must visit ALL specified countries, OR = tour must visit ANY of the specified countries

countries.description: Filter by countries visited DURING the tour itinerary. Use 'start_country'/'end_country' for departure/destination countries.

values.description: City IDs that the tour visits. Use b2b-cities-search to find city IDs (e.g., [1234, 5678])

operator.description: AND = tour must visit ALL specified cities, OR = tour must visit ANY of the specified cities

cities.description: Filter tours by cities visited on the itinerary. City IDs can be obtained from the b2b-cities-search tool

operator_ids.description: Filter tours by specific operator ids. You can add multiple operator ids, then OR operator will be used. For lookup for partner by name, please use algolia-operator-search tool.

currency.description: Currency to return prices in. Use this only when the user explicitly asks for a specific currency. Otherwise the user's default (based on location) is used.

tourradar.name: vertex-tour-title-search
tourradar.title: Tour Title Search
tourradar.description: Search for tours by title using AI-powered semantic search. Returns a list of matching tour IDs and titles. Use this when you need to look up a tour by name. When you know tour id, use b2b-tour-details tool to display details about specific tour

query.description: Tour title or partial title to search for

tourradar.name: vertex-review-search
tourradar.title: Tour Review Search
tourradar.description: Search tour reviews using AI-powered semantic search. Requires tourIds to scope results to specific tours. Use this when the user asks about reviews, feedback, or experiences for specific tours. Combine with an optional text query to find reviews mentioning specific topics (e.g., 'food', 'guide', 'accommodation'). When you don't have tour IDs, use vertex-tour-search or vertex-tour-title-search first to find them.

tourIds.description: Tour IDs to scope reviews to specific tours. Use vertex-tour-title-search to find tour IDs if needed.

query.description: Short natural language search query to find specific review topics. Keep it concise — do NOT expand into keyword lists. Use simple phrases like 'food quality', 'guide', 'hotel issues'. The search engine handles semantic matching automatically. Prefer using 'keywords' parameter instead when user intent maps to specific topics. Leave empty to return all reviews.

keywords.description: Array of keyword phrases to search reviews for. These are joined with OR logic to find reviews matching any of the given topics. Use short phrases (e.g., ['food quality', 'meals', 'restaurant']). When provided, this takes priority over 'query'.

pageSize.description: Number of reviews to return (1-20, default 10)

pageToken.description: Token for fetching the next page of results. Use the nextPageToken value from a previous response.

tourradar.name: web-tour-booking
tourradar.title: Tour Booking
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user explicitly wants to book a tour and has provided their contact details.

Creates a booking request for a specific tour. Requires real contact details (email, names; phone optional). Always confirm the departure date by checking departures first. Never fabricate or assume user data - always ask for missing information.

email.description: Customer email address. If email is not given by the customer, please ask him before executing this endpoint. Never generate email by yourself, always ask customer for give the email before calling this tool.

phoneNumber.description: Customer phone number in E-164 format. Is optional, but recommended to provide it. Never put fake phone number, always ask customer for give the phone number before calling this tool. If customer do not gives phone number, just skip this field

extraInformation.description: Customer extra information from the customer or agent. Fill here if customer has provided any additional information about the booking. This is optional and can be empty.

agentInformation.description: Agent information about the booking. Describe here what customer is looked for, preferences etc, what you can recognize. Will be helpful for our Customer Support team to understand the customer needs.

paxAdults.description: Number of the adults. Always ask customer for this information, is important to have it

url.description: URL to the booking confirmation page, where customer can see the booking details and confirm it. PLEASE ALWAYS SHARE this URL WITH THE CUSTOMER, so he can see the booking details and confirm it.

tourradar.name: web-tour-send-brochure
tourradar.title: Tour Brochure
tourradar.description:
Use this when the user requests a tour brochure or PDF to be sent to their email.

Sends a tour brochure (PDF file) to the specified email address. Before using this, ensure the user has provided their email address. If not provided, ask the user for it. Never generate or assume email addresses.

email.description: User email address. If email is not given by the user, please ask him before executing this endpoint. Never generate email by yourself, always ask user for give the email before calling this tool.

success.description: Success message when brochure is sent

error.description: Error message if brochure sending failed

tourradar.name: algolia-operator-search
tourradar.title: Operator search
tourradar.description:
Use this when you need to find a tour operator's ID by their name for filtering tours.

Searches tour operators by name and returns matching operators with their IDs. Use the returned operator ID with tour search filters.

textSearch.description: Operator name for search, for example `Topdeck`
